title: Read code for resource and durability gaps
description: Inspect systems code and report unclosed resources, missing fsync, unchecked syscalls, and unsafe signal handlers.
body: Use when asked whether systems-level code is correct and robust. Actions: read the code and verify each acquired fd/lock/mapping/child has a deterministic close on all paths, durability-critical writes use temp-fsync-rename-fsync-dir, syscall returns and errno are checked (with EINTR/short-write handling), signal handlers do only async-signal-safe work, and memory/fd use is bounded. Do not assume a returned write is durable or that the happy-path close is enough. Record each gap with the file/line and classify as durability, resource-leak, syscall-handling, signal-safety, or memory. This skill is read-only and must not change behavior without runtime permission and a test. Validation signals include a concrete list of sites and their risk class. Risk boundary: report any missing fsync on a durability path or leaked resource under load as a blocking finding and recommend a crash/leak test.
